name: Scottie Cramp
description: Scottie cramp is a hereditary episodic movement disorder in Scottish Terriers caused by a serotonin metabolism defect. Episodes of muscle over-contraction occur during excitement or exercise, causing abnormal gait and posture but no pain or loss of consciousness.
treatment_overview: Diazepam or acepromazine may reduce episode frequency and severity. Avoiding triggers is the primary management. The condition is not progressive and does not shorten lifespan.
prevention_tips: DNA test available — avoid breeding affected dogs
Reduce exercise intensity and excitement if needed
